rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical vicinal aminotrifluoromethylation of alkenes using CF3SO2Na as CF3 source and acetonitrile as N-nucleophile to give β-trifluoromethylamines via Ritter-type amination.
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of CF3SO2Na releases SO2 and generates CF3 radical; the β-CF3 carbon radical intermediate is further oxidized at the anode to a benzylic carbocation; cathode reduces protons to H2.

key_intermediates.name: nitrilium ion from MeCN attack on carbocation

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes CF3SO2- to CF3 radical (losing SO2); CF3 radical adds to the alkene giving a benzylic C-radical, which is further oxidized at the anode to a benzylic carbocation; MeCN attacks the cation and water hydrolyzes the nitrilium (Ritter-type) to the β-CF3 acetamide; cathode reduces 2H+ to H2.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction suppressed (3a only trace); CF3-TEMPO adduct 4 detected by HRMS ([M+H]+ = 226.1416). Without electricity, TEMPO fully recovered and no product, showing CF3 radical is generated anodically rather than by TEMPO oxidation.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Yield drops to 12%; CF3-BHT adduct 5 ([M+H]+ = 289.1778) and β-CF3-benzyl-BHT adduct 6 ([M+H]+ = 393.2401) detected, supporting both CF3 radical and β-CF3 C-radical intermediates.

probe_reagents.name: D2O (water)
probe_reagents.observation: In ultra-dry MeCN/CH2Cl2 no D-incorporated product; adding 4 equiv H2O (with CD3CN) gives D3-3a in 72% GC yield ([M+H]+ = 235.1124), proving the amide oxygen comes from water and MeCN is the N source.

probe_reagents.name: CD3CN
probe_reagents.observation: D3-labeled acetamide product confirms MeCN incorporation via Ritter-type pathway.

notes: CV shows oxidation peak of CF3SO2Na at 0.89 V vs Ag/AgCl and a higher peak at 0.93 V when styrene is co-present, supporting anodic oxidation of the sulfinate rather than the alkene. Analogous CF2H reaction with CF2HSO2Na gives the β-CF2H amide (3a', 54%), consistent with the same SET/Ritter pathway. X-ray of 3j confirms regiochemistry (CF3 on terminal carbon, NHAc on benzylic carbon).
